message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  Rapid sample-to-answer tests for detection of SARS-CoV-2 are emerging and data on their relative performance is urgently needed. We evaluated the analytical performance of two rapid nucleic acid tests, Cepheid Xpert
                  <jats:sup>®</jats:sup>
                  Xpress SARS-CoV-2 and Mobidiag Novodiag
                  <jats:sup>®</jats:sup>
                  Covid-19, in comparison to a combination reference of three large-scale PCR tests. Moreover, utility of the Novodiag
                  <jats:sup>®</jats:sup>
                  test in tertiary care emergency departments was assessed. In the preliminary evaluation, analysis of 90 respiratory samples resulted in 100% specificity and sensitivity for Xpert
                  <jats:sup>®</jats:sup>
                  , whereas analysis of 107 samples resulted in 93.4% sensitivity and 100% specificity for Novodiag
                  <jats:sup>®</jats:sup>
                  . Rapid SARS-CoV-2 testing with Novodiag
                  <jats:sup>®</jats:sup>
                  was made available for four tertiary care emergency departments in Helsinki, Finland between 18 and 31 May, coinciding with a rapidly declining epidemic phase. Altogether 361 respiratory specimens, together with relevant clinical data, were analyzed with Novodiag
                  <jats:sup>®</jats:sup>
                  and reference tests: 355/361 of the specimens were negative with both methods, and 1/361 was positive in Novodiag
                  <jats:sup>®</jats:sup>
                  and negative by the reference method. Of the 5 remaining specimens, two were negative with Novodiag
                  <jats:sup>®</jats:sup>
                  , but positive with the reference method with late Ct values. On average, a test result using Novodiag
                  <jats:sup>®</jats:sup>
                  was available nearly 8 hours earlier than that obtained with the large-scale PCR tests. While the performance of novel sample-to-answer PCR tests need to be carefully evaluated, they may provide timely and reliable results in detection of SARS-CoV-2 and thus facilitate patient management including effective cohorting.
                </jats:p>
title: SARS-CoV-2 sample-to-answer nucleic acid testing in a tertiary care emergency department: evaluation and utility

reference.article-title: Laboratory diagnosis of emerging human coronavirus infections - the state of the art

reference.unstructured: Assennato SM , Ritchie AV , Nadala C , Goel N , Zhang H , Datir R , Gupta RK , Curran MD , Lee HH . 2020. Performance evaluation of the point-of-care SAMBA II SARS-CoV-2 Test for detection of SARS-CoV-2. medRxiv 2020.05.24.20100990 (Preprint). doi: https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.05.24.20100990.

Ghofrani M , Casas MT , Pelz RK , Kroll C , Blum N , Foster SD . 2020. Performance characteristics of the ID NOW COVID-19 assay: A regional health care system experience. medRxiv 2020.06.03.20116327 (Preprint). doi: https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.06.03.20116327.
